The 2026 Primary Election will be held on:
Tuesday, May 19, 2026
Polls will be open from -
NOTICE OF COMPUTATION AND CANVASSING FOR THE PRIMARY ELECTION HELD MAY 19, 2026
Notice is hereby given that the Crawford County Board of Elections intends to begin a pre-canvass of all mail in and absentee ballots at , prevailing time, on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, in the Assembly Room of the Courthouse. Ballot envelopes will be opened, and the ballots will be readied for tabulation on scanners to be tallied after . At no time will information from the ballots be discussed or disseminated until the canvass is complete.
The last day to register to vote or make changes to your registration before the Primary is:
Monday, May 4, 2026
The last day to apply for a Mail-in or Absentee Ballot before the Primary is:
Tuesday, May 12, 2026
WE ARE IN NEED OF POLL WORKERS TO WORK UPCOMING ELECTIONS!
Poll workers work for the entire day on Election Day, from before the time the polls open at , until after the polls close at . You are compensated for working on Election Day.
Help check-in voters, manage the lines, and make sure voters know where to go at each step in the voting process.
Work together to manage the polling place, keep track of the number of voters, and make sure that the returns are delivered to the county election office at the end of the day.
If you are interested in being a Poll Worker on Election Day, please call our office at: 814-333-7307.

About
The County of Crawford lies in northwestern Pennsylvania. Originally it was inhabited by the Iroquiois Indians and other tribes and nations. In later years, the land was acquired by a group of Dutch investors under title of the Holland Land Company and resold to settlers.
